When AI Starts Taking Action for Us,
What Will Be the Most Important Human Ability?
Recently, more and more conversations have focused on Agent AI.
From searching for information,
organizing knowledge,
and planning our schedules,
to eventually assisting with increasingly complex tasks.
For thousands of years, humans have built tools to extend our abilities.
But now, we are creating a new kind of tool.
One that does not only follow instructions,
but can understand intentions, evaluate options, and take actions on our behalf.
This is exciting.
But it also reminds me to ask myself:
As AI becomes better at organizing information,
analyzing choices,
and even suggesting directions,
will I still take the time to think:
What do I truly want?
AI may generate countless possibilities.
But choosing which path is worth pursuing still comes from human values,
experiences,
responsibility,
and judgment.
Perhaps the most important question in the AI era is not how much AI can do.
It is this:
As AI gains greater ability to act,
will humans continue to cultivate their own ability to judge?
Because the future should not be determined only by the most efficient answer.
It should be shaped by what humans choose to value.
